title: Writing code is cheap now - Agentic Engineering Patterns
reason: Deep, insightful analysis of how AI coding agents change software development economics and engineering practices.

title: FreeBSD doesn't have Wi-Fi driver for my old MacBook. AI build one for me
reason: Outstanding technical case study on using AI agents to develop a kernel driver with detailed methodology.

title: What's so hard about continuous learning?
reason: Excellent technical depth on why LLM continuous learning is difficult—covers training mechanics, safety risks, fine-tuning failures.

title: Customizing the ways the dialog manager dismisses itself: Detecting the ESC key, second (failed) attempt
reason: Classic Raymond Chen deep-dive into Windows API internals. Exceptional technical depth on message handling and dialog management.

title: Where Do Specifications Fit in the Dependency Tree?
reason: Deep, comprehensive analysis of how specifications (RFCs, standards) relate to package dependencies. Novel perspective.

title: Some Silly Z3 Scripts I Wrote
reason: High-quality technical content on Z3 SMT solver and formal methods from a recognized expert.

title: Ladybird adopts Rust, with help from AI
reason: Excellent technical case study on using AI coding agents to port a JavaScript engine from C++ to Rust.

title: go-concurrent-map-bench: Benchmarks for concurrent hash map implementations in Go
reason: Excellent technical depth with comprehensive benchmarks comparing Go concurrent map implementations.

title: Which web frameworks are most token-efficient for AI agents?
reason: Original research testing 19 web frameworks with Claude Code to measure token efficiency. Rigorous methodology.

title: Scent, In Silico
reason: Excellent deep dive into AI/ML for olfaction—covers Google's Principal Odor Map, Graph Neural Networks, Osmo's generative fragrance AI.
